claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child I'd like to claim for. My son Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Now I have a few more questions to help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Now I need to understand your relationship with Alex:

**Does Alex live with you, or if he doesn't live with you, are you paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after him (for example, on food, clothes or pocket money)?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just a few more questions to ensure I have all the information needed:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Just one more question to complete my assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or any other living arrangement away from home for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he has been in care for a short while, but it's only been a couple of months.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for sharing that information. I need to understand this situation a bit better as it affects eligibility.

**How many weeks has Alex been in care? And does he spend any regular time at home each week (for example, specific nights or days)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been about 7 weeks now. And no, he doesn't come home regularly - he's there full-time at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. That's very helpful.

Since Alex has been in care for 7 weeks (which is under the 8-week limit), and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, let me assess your eligibility.

**One final question: Are you a UK resident with the right to reside here, or do you have any immigration status I should be aware of (such as pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a UK resident, yes. I have the right to reside here - no issues with immigration status.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's the assessment:

**For Alex (8 years old): ELIGIBLE**

You meet all the requirements:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live in the UK with the right to reside
- Alex normally lives with you, and you're responsible for him
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is within the 8-week period during which you can continue to receive Child Benefit (as long as nobody else claims)

**Important to note:** After 8 weeks in care, you would usually no longer be entitled to Child Benefit unless certain exceptions apply (such as if Alex spends at least 24 hours a week at home). Since Alex is currently at 7 weeks, you should be aware that your eligibility may change after the 8-week mark if the situation continues and no exceptions apply.

If Alex's circumstances change (for example, if he starts coming home regularly or if the care arrangement ends), please make sure to update HMRC.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Alex normally lives with the claimant, establishing that the claimant is responsible for him. Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week threshold specified in the rules. According to the guidance, Child Benefit usually continues for 8 weeks after a child goes into care if nobody else claims. Since it has only been 7 weeks and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the claimant remains eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is within the 8-week period during which Child Benefit continues if nobody else is claiming. The claimant meets all other eligibility criteria: they live in the UK with the right to reside, they are responsible for Alex (who normally lives with them), and no one else is claiming for him.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK and has the right to reside here. Alex normally lives with the claimant, establishing that the claimant is responsible for him. Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is under the 8-week threshold specified in the rules. According to the guidance, Child Benefit usually continues for 8 weeks after a child goes into care if nobody else claims. Since it has only been 7 weeks and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, the claimant remains eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Although Alex has been in care for 7 weeks, this is within the 8-week period during which Child Benefit continues if nobody else is claiming. The claimant meets all other eligibility criteria: they live in the UK with the right to reside, they are responsible for Alex (who normally lives with them), and no one else is claiming for him.
